one_time_description: Share of income from First Nation controlled entities (Lytton First Nation Economic Development Corporation and Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP) recorded through modified equity method
economic_notes: Four government business enterprises: Kumcheen Enterprises Ltd., Lytton Development Corp., Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP, Lytton First Nation Economic Development Corporation. Community severely impacted by Lytton Creek wildfire June 30, 2021 which destroyed 5 band buildings and 39 houses on three reserves plus band office. Ongoing rebuilding efforts.
notes: Deferred revenue increased significantly from $3.24M to $17.26M driven by large ISC capital project deferrals ($9.58M). Share of income from GBEs of $5.37M is a large non-cash equity pickup primarily from Lytton First Nation Economic Development Corporation ($2.08M) and Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP ($854K net income). OSR is negative because total government transfers exceed total revenue when netting deferred revenue. Lytton First Nation was devastated by the June 2021 Lytton Creek wildfire; rebuilding costs and ISC funding reflect ongoing recovery. No remuneration schedule included in the provided financial statements. Social Development and Health are combined as a single segment in the statements. Capital Programs expenses separately include Capital Fund amortization of $1,871,224. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($39,261,511) + FNHA ($1,594,897) + CMHC ($634,991) + Province of BC ($770,233) + Dept of Justice ($121,080) + Community Partners Legal Aid ($23,942) + First Nations Education Steering Committee ($262,973) + First Nations Land Management ($145,890) + BC Hydro ($70,259) + Works program ($11,970) = $42,897,746 before deferred revenue adjustment; OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers.
